observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We hereby recommended the following:
- Concerned officials shall give utmost importance to proper procurement
planning where actual needs, right quantity and right processes, among
others are considered. Further, procurement above the threshold shall be
done thru procurement service or public bidding to foster transparency and
competitiveness and ensure the prices are the most advantageous to the
municipality
- Refrain from paying purchases out of the cash advance of the Municipal
Treasurer unless circumstances warrant its application.
- Discontinue the grant of cash advance on account of infrastructure or
undertakings on project basis.
- Stop the practice of splitting requisition and purchase order. Items needed
for one quarter and/or single activity/project shall be consolidated and
procured in one purchase order.
- Instruct Municipal Accountant to issue certification stating therein that
previous cash advance has been liquidated and accounted for in the book to
support additional claim of cash advance together with other liquidation
documents; otherwise, a grant of additional cash advance shall not be
processed and paid.
- Submit a written justification explaining therein the reasons for said
payments despite violation of existing regulations.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended that management shall undertake the following:
- Ensure that all procurements shall pass through the regular mode of
procurement to promote fair competition in accordance with R.A. 9184.
- All efforts shall be exerted to require suppliers to submit the needed
documentations otherwise; transactions covered by it shall be disallowed in
audit, since contracts were awarded to suppliers who are disqualified to enter
into government contract.
- Refrain from undertaking a project not supported with complete bid and
technical documentation. However, awarding of contract shall only be made
to a legally, financially and technically capable supplier/contractor and
willing to cooperate in the submission of required documents.
- Submit a written justification explaining therein the reasons for said
payments despite violation of existing regulation.

Recommendations:
We recommended to management the following:
- Determine the whereabouts of these checks and provide us of its status
immediately thereafter.
- The Municipal Treasurer through the Municipal Accountant shall submit to
COA all checks that has been reported as cancelled.
- Extra effort shall be exerted in locating the missing checks. However, if
despite of all the efforts and diligent search to recover the missing checks, but
still in vain, immediately apply for a relief of accountability and submit
justification letter for late application to COA, together with the above-
mentioned documents as prescribed in section COA Circular No. 93-404
dated October 18, 1993 and COA Circular No. 84-233 dated August 21, 1984.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended the following:
- Existing practice of the agency shall be discontinued as this is not the proper
way of processing claims and it may further create adverse impact to agency
and to concerned officials if this will continue to exist in their transactions.
- Submit a written justification explaining therein the reasons for processing
claims without complete and appropriate documentation.
- The Municipal Accountant shall likewise submit all the required
documentation needed to establish the validity and legality of the
disbursements and henceforth, payment shall only be made for claims
supported with complete documentation.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended the following:
- The Municipal Treasurer shall render his monthly reports such as
Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) with supporting documents, Payrolls,
Official Receipts and other liquidation documents including revenue reports
and supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant on or before the 5th
day of the ensuing month to allow its prompt recording in the books of
accounts.
- The Municipal Accountant shall coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to
ensure prompt submission of DVs and other related reports, undertake
recording in the books of accounts, and submit the same within 10 days of
the ensuing month to the Office of the Auditor.
- Concerned officials shall prioritize and adopt measures to address issues on
delayed submission of reports to be able to meet the deadline set and be free
from possible sanctions may be imposed from violating thereof as required
under article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.
- Require concerned official/s to submit written justification explaining
therein the reasons for late submission, and if not satisfied, appropriate
actions shall be instituted against the accountable officers for the
delayed/non submission of financial reports as required under Article 218 of
the Revised Penal Code.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended to management the following:
- To faithfully comply and enforce the periodic submission of these reports
within the prescribed period. Concerned officials shall likewise prioritize
submission of these reports and look into the root cause of these delays and
immediately initiate measures to address the problem.
- Require concern official/s to submit written justification explaining therein
the reasons of late submission, and if not satisfied, appropriate actions shall
be instituted against the accountable officers for the delayed/non
submission of financial reports as required under Article 218 of the Revised
Penal Code.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer shall faithfully adhere to the
provisions stated in the afore-mentioned regulation in relation to the deduction,
withholding and remittance of required taxes specifically on goods and services,
and remit the same within the required timeframe which is every 10th of the
ensuing month.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended the following:
- Intensify efforts for the timely conduct of annual physical count of PPE and
submit the required Reports on PPE to COA within deadline set.
- Invite representative from COA to witness the said inventory taking.
Invitation letter shall be sent three (3) days prior to the scheduled date to
give ample time to prepare and settle whatever time inconveniences that may
have.
- Prioritize the update of the property and accounting records including PPE
subsidiary ledger, and immediately present the same to the office of the
auditor for verification and review.

Recommendations:
We recommended to management the following:
- The Municipal Treasurer shall prepare separate cashbook for the two (2)
bank accounts under General Fund reflecting therein all checks issued in
chronological order including cancelled ones.
- Report of Checks Issued (RCI) shall be prepared for every depository
account to comply with existing regulations and facilitate easy conduct of
cash examination and post audit as well.
- Maintain and update check registers to monitor checks issued and status of
its releases to respective payees.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended the following:
- The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer shall exert
more efforts to maintain their respective accounting and budget records,
using the prescribed form, and update the same regularly.
- The necessity of additional workforce, who has the required expertise and
qualification, for the two (2) offices shall be properly considered and shall
be prioritized as well, including their attendance to trainings/seminars to
keep them abreast of the latest accounting and budget issuances.

Recommendations:
We recommended to management the following:
- Submit all other transaction documents and so we could complete our audit
on GAD Funds and refrain from processing GAD transaction not duly
supported with documents.
- Immediately submit all other requirements in settlement of audit suspension
to avoid disallowance in audit; and it will facilitate completion of the audit on
GAD fund as well.

Recommendations:
The management shall undertake the following:
- Immediately submit all the lacking requirements in settlement of the audit
suspension to avoid disallowance in audit; and facilitate completion of the
audit on LDRRM fund as well.
- The unutilized balance of the LDRRM shall be immediately transferred to
the LDRRM special trust fund account and provide our office with the
necessary document to evidence the said fund transfer.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended the following:
- The management shall faithfully follow correct disbursement system and so
only valid and proper claims will be processed and paid.
- Exert all possible efforts to settle all audit suspensions within the required
timeframe to avoid disallowance in audit, and the required project validation
and ocular inspection could be promptly undertaken as well.

observations.recommendation:
- The Municipal Accountant shall not process claims/payments not duly
supported with required documents. She shall likewise follow correct
disbursement system at all times.
- Exert all possible efforts to settle all audit suspensions within the required
timeframe to avoid disallowance in audit.
- Be guided of the pertinent sections in the afore-mentioned regulations and in
other GPPB issued resolutions simplifying requirements in project
implementation during emergency cases.

- The Municipal Accountant shall prioritize submission of the COVID related
reports and transactions documents since the prescribed period within which
to submit has already lapsed, and the necessity of its immediate audit has
been laid down to establish proper utilization thereof.

- The Accounting, Supply and Property Units shall update regularly, PPE
Subsidiary Ledgers, Stock Cards and Property Cards, respectively, and
reconcile balances of these records with the Report on Physical Count of
Inventories and PPE to be able to reflect a complete and accurate balances in
the financial statements.
- The management shall likewise include in their yearly plan the conduct of the
required physical count of Inventories and PPE to be undertaken by the
agency’s Inventory Committee, and so required reports can be prepared and
submitted to COA on or before January 31 of the ensuing year.
- Invite representative from the Commission on Audit (COA) to act as witness
in every inventory-taking to comply with the regulation. Invitation letter
shall be served and stamped received by COA at least three days prior to the
scheduled date to give the auditor an ample time to prepare and settle
whatever time inconveniences that may have.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer shall faithfully adhere to the
provisions stated in the afore-mentioned regulation in relation to the deduction,
withholding and remittance of required taxes specifically on goods and services,
and remit the same within the required timeframe which is every 10th of the
ensuing month.

- The management shall faithfully follow correct disbursement system and so
only valid and proper claims will be processed and paid.
- Payments in whatever nature shall be made by check, directly to
suppliers/creditors and not thru cash advance in the name of the Municipal
Treasurer. However, if direct payment to creditor/supplier is impractical, it
may be paid by the disbursing officer in the form of cash out of his cash
advance to conform to existing regulations.
- Exert all possible efforts to settle all audit suspensions within the required
timeframe to avoid disallowance in audit, and the required ocular inspection
could be promptly undertaken as well.
- Be guided of the pertinent sections in the afore-mentioned regulations and
in other GPPB issued resolutions simplifying requirements in project
implementation during emergency cases.

Take heed to the required guidelines on proper documentation as well as on the
prescribed timelines on reports submission, especially those transactions
involving credit financing as usage of the loan amount and payment of
amortization are strictly monitored, since the amount is substantial that
improper documentation thereof would expose government interest to possible
risk and may result to further loss of government funds.

- Exert all possible efforts to collate all the lacking documents and submit the
same to COA immediately to avoid disallowance in audit.
- Refrain from undertaking a project not supported with complete bid and
technical documentation. However, awarding of contract shall only be made
to a legally, financially and technically capable supplier/contractor and
willing to cooperate in the submission of required documents.
- Assign additional qualified personnel to take charge in the preparation of
documents and settlement of all audit suspensions to be able to meet the
required deadlines.

We hereby recommended that management shall cause the preparation and
submission of the required Monthly Reports of Official Travels and Monthly
Reports of Fuel Consumption to comply with the foregoing regulations, and to
facilitate proper control and accounting of the fuel expense, and establish
reasonableness of fuel consumption as well.

The Municipal Accountant shall maintain special accounts in the general fund
specifically for 20% Development Fund, Loans and Economic Enterprise
through preparation and maintenance of complete subsidiary ledgers including
separate SL for loan payable, expenditures and interest payments for every
loan obtained, as this will facilitate detection of unrecorded loan releases,
principal and interest repayment, and interest charges. Moreover, she shall also
prepare Financial Statements (FS) for special accounts and submit the same to
COA as an attachment to the FS for General Fund.

- Prioritize submission of the monthly report of transactions to COA within
the deadline set to avoid negative repercussion; and timely audit of its
transactions could be undertaken as well.
- Maximize work assignment among accounting personnel and/or assign
additional staff, if the present workforce is not enough, so as to prevent work
overload and meet deadlines on reports submission.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer shall stamp “PAID” all DVs
and its supporting documents after payment was made, to prevent the re-use of
the same on similar transaction.

- The Municipal Accountant ensures that the required supporting documents
of all claims are complete before affixing her signature in Box A of the
Disbursement Voucher.
- The management shall likewise observe that proper flow of transactions is
strictly followed so that only valid and proper claims are processed and
paid.

- The management shall verify status of its approval with the National Solid
Waste Management Commission, if already submitted to NSWMC, and so,
deficiencies could be noted, and submission of the lacking documents could
be immediately made.
- Complete other required documentation/data on Solid Waste Management
and immediately submit copy of the same to COA including the creation of
the Solid Waste Management Board and proof in the establishment of the
required solid waste facilities.
- Fully comply with the Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 and other
regulations pertaining thereto to be able to have a systematic and
comprehensive ecological waste management program.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to management the following:
- The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), BAC Secretariat and the agency’s
Technical Working Group (TWG) shall adhere to R.A 9184 and to other
procurement rules and regulations in all their procurement undertakings.
- Submit all lacking documentation to enable us complete our audit on the said
fund and further undertake the required ocular inspection. Moreover,
refrain from processing claims/payments neither supported with complete
documentation nor included in the Annual Investment Plan unless duly
justified, so as to avoid audit suspension and disallowance.
- Observe deadlines in reports submission for a timely audit and inspection,
and immediate inclusion of its results in the Annual Audit report.

We recommended to management the following:
- The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), BAC Secretariat and the agency’s
Technical Working Group (TWG) shall adhere to R.A 9184 and to other
procurement rules and regulations in all their procurement undertakings.
- The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer shall discontinue
processing of claims/payments for LDRRM related projects and activities
without appropriate documentation so as to avoid audit suspension and
disallowance.
- Concerned officials shall submit all lacking documentation to enable us
complete our audit on the said fund.

We recommended to management the following:
- The Municipal Treasurer through the Municipal Accountant shall submit
to COA all checks that has been reported as cancelled.
- Ensure that all Report of Checks Issued shall bear copy of cancelled
checks (if there is any) to comply with the above-mentioned regulation.
However, if these checks could no longer be found despite diligent search
and effort, these have to be immediately reported to Head of Agency and
to COA, and likewise, submit the afore-mentioned required reports to
COA immediately thereafter.

We hereby recommended the following measures:
- The Municipal Treasurer shall coordinate with the Municipal Assessor in
 the preparation of the required Certified List of Taxpayers and immediately
provide copy of the same to the Municipal Accountant to be able to establish
accurate amount of basic RPT and SET receivables.
- Upon receipt of the Latest Certified List of Taxpayers, the Municipal
Accountant shall make necessary adjusting entries to correct the recorded
amount of RPT/SET Receivable vis a vis the submitted list of taxpayers.
- The accounting office shall likewise maintain an index card for each
taxpayer to facilitate the establishment of the accurate amount of
collectibles from each taxpayer.
- Regularly update RPT/SET Receivable accounts Subsidiary Ledgers.

We recommended to management to enforce collection strategies and remedies
to increase RPT/SET collections as well as undertake measures to require all
delinquent taxpayers to prioritize payment of tax on Real Property within the
deadlines and in accordance with existing regulations.

We recommended that:
- The management shall always formulate GAD Plan with great emphasis
on proper planning and compliance to various GAD policies and
regulations.
- Fully implement budgeted amount for GAD to be able to meet its
minimum requirement.
- The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer shall discontinue
processing of claims/payments for GAD related projects and activities
without appropriate documentation so as to avoid audit suspension and
disallowance.
- Concerned officials shall immediately submit all lacking documentations
to enable us complete our audit on the said fund.

- Fast track finalization of the Solid Waste Management (SWM) Plan and
have it approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission to
ensure that programs, projects and activities included in the plan are in
accordance with the National Solid Waste Management Framework.
- Complete other required documentation/data on Solid Waste Management
and submit copy to COA immediately thereafter.
- Ensure that proper mechanisms, like budget and manpower, are in place to
give assurance to proper implementation of the plan.
- Solid waste management programs/projects must align with the approved
SWM Plan. Any deviation from the said plan could be a ground for
disallowance in audit.
- Fully comply with the Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 and other
regulations pertaining thereto to be able to have a systematic and
comprehensive ecological waste management program.

We hereby recommended to take careful planning in the preparation of its
respective plans following existing laws, rules and regulations and with great
consideration on the priority needs of the constituents; and ensure that every
programs and activities incorporated therein are viable for implementation.
We further recommended to submit immediately all the required
documentation relative to its successful implementation together with the breakdown of its actual expenditures and other necessary data, to facilitate the
conduct of our post-audit activity.

We recommended the following:
- Discontinue payment of claims not supported with complete
documentations.
- Observe correct disbursement system.
- Exert all possible efforts to settle all audit suspensions within the required
timeframe to avoid disallowance in audit.
- Assign additional qualified personnel to take charge in the settlement of all
audit suspensions to be able to meet the required deadlines.

We hereby recommended to management the following:
- Head of Agency shall request and/or demand in writing from the Municipal
Treasurer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions and
submit the same together with other supporting documents to the
Accounting Section within the time prescribed by the regulation.
- Head of the accounting section shall prioritize submission of transaction
documents and other financial reports to COA and maximize work
assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent work overload
and meet deadlines on reports submission.
- Take heed to the prescribed timeframe on submission of accounts and
financial reports to avoid sanctions that may be imposed for violating
thereof as required under section 127 of P.D. 1445, which provides that:
Administrative disciplinary actions:
Subject to rules and regulations as may be approved by the president,
any unjustified failure by the public officer concerned to comply with
any requirement imposed in this Code shall constitute neglect of duty
and shall be a ground for administrative disciplinary action against
the said public officer who, with the degree of his guilt in accordance
with the civil service law. Repeated unjustified failure to comply with
the requirements impose in this Code shall be conclusive proof the
public officer concerned is notoriously undesirable.

We hereby recommended that Bids and Awards Committee shall undertake
the following:
- Comply with the provisions stated in the procurement law specifically on
section 13.1 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184, to enhance transparency in the
procurement process; and likewise make it a valid procurement.
- Take note of the five (5) day requirement wherein invitation should have been sent to observers.
- Invite two (2) observers, in addition to COA representative, of known
qualifications and possesses the required expertise relevant to goods/services
being procured.

The management shall undertake the following:
- Comply with the required laws, rules and regulations on LDRRM to avoid
sanction that may be imposed from violating thereof.
- The Municipal Budget Officer shall prepare and maintain separate
LDRRM Registries of Appropriation, Allotments and Obligations for QRF,
MOOE and Capital Outlay as required in the afore-mentioned regulation.
- The Municipal Accountant shall also prepare and maintain subsidiary
records for LDRRM and make it available to COA for the necessary review
and verification; and provide adequate disclosure in the Notes to Financial
Statements.

Municipal Budget Officer shall always takes heed to the requirement of the law
and ensure that budget records were updated regularly to be able to provide
reliable information towards fair presentation of agency’s Financial
Statements.

We hereby recommended to management the following:
- Prepare the required AAPSI within the deadline set, which is within 60 days
upon receipt of the Annual Audit Report, following the correct format and
incorporating therein all audit findings and recommendations stated in Part
II of the said report.
- Submit immediately the revised copy of the AAPSI for CY 2018 to enable us
validates its contents and prepare corresponding report as well.
- Provide copy of the said AAPSI to DBM, Speaker of the House of
Representatives, House Committee on Appropriations and Senate Committee
on Finance, as mandated in section 99 of R.A. 11260.
- Posting of the said report in the agency’s website is likewise enjoined.

Per records, material amount of audit suspensions which we require settlement thru submission of appropriate supporting documents has remained unsettled as of this date despite of our demand letters, tracers and verbal reminders. 


Concerned official reasoned out that the primary cause of this deficiency is that people assigned to perform the job are not enough considering the volume and complexities of the paper works involved and the difficulties in collating all the documents needed to support the disbursements. 

Moreover, all unsettled suspensions especially those nearly maturing into disallowance if not settled immediately shall become a disallowance pursuant to section 82 of P.D. 1445 and Section 9 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

 Section 82 of P.D. 1445 provides that:

The auditor concerned shall, at convenient intervals, send a written notice under the certificate of settlement to each officer whose accounts have been audited and settled in whole or in part by him, stating the balances found due thereon and certified , and the charges or differences arising from the settlement by reason of disallowances, charges, or suspensions. The certificate shall be properly itemized and shall state the reasons for disallowance, charge, or suspension of credit. A charge of suspension which is not satisfactorily explained within ninety days after receipts of the certificate or notice by the accountable officer concerned shall become a disallowance, unless the commission or auditor concerned shall, in writing and for good cause shown extend the time for answer beyond ninety days.

Section 9 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 states that:

…… a suspension should be settled within ninety (90) calendar days from receipt of the NS; otherwise the transaction covered by it shall be disallowed/charged after the auditor shall have satisfied himself that such action is appropriate. Consequently, the audit shall issue the corresponding ND/NC…….

Procurement above the threshold should be done thru procurement service or public bidding pursuant to the provisions under RA 9184 to foster transparency and competitiveness and ensure the prices are the most advantageous to the municipality. However, splitting by dividing the projects into artificial phases to circumvent the regulations should not be resorted to.

Stop the payment of purchases out of the cash advance of the municipal treasurer and reimbursement basis unless circumstances warrant its application.
observations.reason:
Implementation was undertaken in a gradual manner. However, they assured us to fully comply with the deficiency in CY 2016.

Procurement of supplies and materials under 20% LDF and Trust Fund were already undertaken thru competitive bidding. However, supplies and materials for  minor repairs and other commonly used supplies under General Fund were still procured thru local suppliers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management shall include in their CY 2015 action plan to be registered with PhilGeps to be able to fully utilize the PhilGeps and meets its objective on  promoting transparency and efficiency in   procurement process and to give all interested parties an equal opportunity to bid.

Prioritize the initiation of physical count on all PPE as this will help concerned officials in the proper discharge of their duties and responsibilities towards fair presentation of the agency’s financial statements.    

Create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the overall physical count on PPE and submit corresponding reports to the office of the auditor immediately thereafter.

The accounting office and treasury office shall likewise maintain and update an Equipment Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively and oftentimes reconcile balances of each cards maintained by them and with the balances in the Report on Physical Count to be able to reflect complete and accurate PPE balance in the financial statements.

Direct official concerned to limit payment thru reimbursement basis in a manner as required by law to avoid disallowance in audit.

Comply to the requirement on the principle of impress system; that all payment must be paid by check except those that cannot be conveniently paid by check.
